text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Osei-Bonsu, Margaret
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Hepatobiliary Surgery
CLINICAL HISTORY: 66-year-old female with known borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) of the head and uncinate process, AJCC clinical stage T3 N1 M0 (Stage IIB), diagnosed by EUS-guided fine needle biopsy. Neoadjuvant treatment with modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles; cycles 7-8 converted to FOLFIRI backbone due to oxaliplatin-related grade 3 neuropathy) followed by consolidation chemoradiation (IMRT 54 Gy in 28 fractions with concurrent capecitabine). Pre-surgical CA 19-9 52 U/mL. Now submitted for evaluation of curative-intent resection specimen.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, pancreatic head and uncinate process, post-neoadjuvant therapy.

OPERATION: Open pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (PPPD, Whipple procedure) with en bloc segmental resection of the superior mesenteric vein (SMV) and primary end-to-end venous reconstruction.

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  Specimen A: Pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple) resection specimen
  Specimen B: Separate superior mesenteric vein segment (en bloc)
  Specimen C: Intraoperative frozen section — pancreatic neck margin (reported separately; see below)
  Specimen D: Intraoperative frozen section — common bile duct margin (reported separately; see below)
  Specimen E: Pericholedochal lymph node, station 12 (submitted separately by surgeon)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTRAOPERATIVE FROZEN SECTION DIAGNOSIS (Specimens C and D):
  Specimen C — Pancreatic neck margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. Viable pancreatic parenchyma with mild acinar atrophy; no carcinoma identified.
  Specimen D — Common bile duct margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. Bile duct epithelium with mild reactive changes; no carcinoma identified.
  [Permanent sections confirm intraoperative frozen section diagnoses.]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

1. PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY SPECIMEN (Specimen A + B):
   MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA WITH EXTENSIVE TREATMENT EFFECT
   - Primary site: Pancreatic head and uncinate process
   - Histologic type: Ductal adenocarcinoma, conventional type (pancreatobiliary pattern)
   - Histologic grade: Moderately differentiated (Grade 2)
   - Tumor size: 2.1 cm in greatest dimension (gross and microscopic)
   - Tumor regression grade (College of American Pathologists, CAP): Grade 2 — Moderate response
     (Residual cancer with evident tumor regression; fibrosis and acellular mucin pools predominate in areas, but more than single cells or rare small groups of cancer cells remain)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Perineural invasion: PRESENT (multiple foci, large-caliber nerves within the uncinate process and peripancreatic soft tissue)
   - Tumor extension: Into peripancreatic soft tissue
   - Duodenal wall involvement: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Common bile duct involvement: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Ampulla of Vater involvement: NOT IDENTIFIED

2. SURGICAL MARGINS:
   - Pancreatic neck (transection) margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (clearance 3 mm, confirmed on permanent section)
   - Common bile duct (proximal) margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (clearance 5 mm)
   - Retroperitoneal / uncinate / SMA margin (posterior surface): NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (closest approach 1.2 mm; R0 resection per CAP and NCCN criteria, i.e., >0 mm clearance)
   - Anterior pancreatic surface (non-peritonealized): NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (clearance 4 mm)
   - SMV groove margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (clearance 2 mm, following en bloc vascular resection; Specimen B)
   - Proximal duodenal (pyloric) margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA
   - Distal duodenal / jejunal margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA

   OVERALL MARGIN STATUS: R0 (all surgical margins negative for invasive carcinoma)

3. REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
   - Total lymph nodes examined: 22
   - Lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma: 1 of 22 (1/22)
   - Location of positive node: Peripancreatic (superior head region)
   - Size of largest metastatic deposit: 0.4 cm
   - Extranodal extension: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Lymph nodes negative: 21 of 22 (including pericholedochal lymph node, Specimen E: negative)

4. PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, Post-Treatment):
   - Primary tumor (ypT): ypT3 — Tumor extends beyond the pancreas into peripancreatic soft tissue; no involvement of celiac axis or SMA
   - Regional lymph nodes (ypN): ypN1 — Metastasis in 1 to 3 regional lymph nodes
   - Distant metastasis (M): Not applicable (M0, no distant metastasis in resection specimen)
   - PATHOLOGIC STAGE GROUP: ypT3 ypN1 M0 — Stage IIB (post-neoadjuvant therapy designation)

5. ADDITIONAL PATHOLOGIC FINDINGS:
   - Pancreatic parenchyma (uninvolved): Moderate acinar atrophy with interlobular fibrosis, consistent with obstructive pancreatitis and treatment effect. Islets of Langerhans are preserved but reduced in number.
   - Duodenum: Mild villous blunting and focal cryptitis, consistent with radiation effect. No dysplasia or malignancy.
   - Common bile duct wall: Periductal fibrosis with mild reactive epithelial changes; no dysplasia or residual carcinoma in situ.
   - Pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN): PanIN-1A/1B identified incidentally in uninvolved pancreatic parenchyma; no high-grade PanIN at the neck margin.
   - Acellular mucin pools: Present within fibrotic treatment bed; no viable tumor cells identified within these pools (scored as treatment effect, not as viable tumor).
   - SMV resection segment (Specimen B): Vessel wall with perivascular fibrosis and chronic inflammation; no carcinoma involvement of the vessel intima or media. Margins of vascular segment: negative.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A is received fresh and then fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number and designated "pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple) specimen." The specimen consists of the pancreatic head and uncinate process, distal common bile duct, a segment of duodenum (approximately 18 cm in length, pylorus-preserving), gallbladder, and peripancreatic soft tissue. The specimen is oriented by the surgical team with a short suture on the superior mesenteric vein (SMV) groove margin and a long suture marking the retroperitoneal (SMA) margin prior to inking.

External appearance: The specimen measures 14.0 x 9.0 x 5.5 cm in aggregate. The serosal surface of the duodenum is smooth and glistening without adhesions or deposits. The peripancreatic soft tissue is firm and indurated consistent with treatment effect. The specimen is inked according to a standard multicolor margin protocol: retroperitoneal/SMA margin (black), SMV groove margin (blue), anterior surface (yellow), posterior surface (green), and bile duct margin (red).

Serial sectioning: Upon serial axial sectioning of the pancreatic head, a firm, white-yellow, stellate, ill-defined mass is identified within the pancreatic head and uncinate process. The mass measures 2.1 cm in greatest dimension (2.1 x 1.8 x 1.5 cm). The cut surface shows a densely fibrotic, gritty, pale yellow-white lesion with areas of central chalky-white calcification (approximately 0.3 cm focus) and a small central area of softening/necrosis (0.2 cm). Compared to the clinical and radiologic pre-treatment tumor size of 3.8 cm, the identified tumor area is substantially smaller, consistent with treatment response. The tumor is located predominantly in the head and uncinate process. The pancreatic duct upstream (body/tail side of neck margin) is mildly dilated at approximately 3 mm (improved from pre-treatment 6.5-7 mm), consistent with resolution of obstruction following stent placement and treatment.

Margin assessment (gross):
- The closest approach of the mass to the retroperitoneal/SMA margin is estimated at 1.0-1.5 mm.
- The SMV groove margin (Specimen B) is not involved macroscopically.
- The pancreatic neck margin is grossly unremarkable without visible tumor.
- The common bile duct lumen is patent with a biliary stent tract present (metallic stent previously removed at time of surgery; stent tract is patent and lined by reactive mucosa).

Gallbladder: Present, 7.5 x 3.2 cm. The serosa is smooth. The mucosa shows mild cholesterolosis. No stones identified in gallbladder lumen. Cystic duct margin is negative (separately submitted).

Duodenum: The duodenal mucosa is grossly intact without ulceration or discrete lesions. The ampulla of Vater is identified at 4.5 cm from the pyloric ring; the ampullary orifice is patent.

Lymph nodes: Peripancreatic and periduodenal fibrofatty tissue is entirely submitted for lymph node examination. Representative sections and entirely submitted fat pads yield a total of 22 lymph nodes ranging from 0.3 to 1.1 cm in greatest dimension.

Specimen B: The superior mesenteric vein segment measures 2.5 cm in length x 0.8 cm in diameter. The external adventitia shows fibrosis and focal areas of fibrous adhesion where the specimen was adherent to the tumor bed. The lumen is patent. The vessel wall is firm. Transverse sections submitted en face for margin evaluation.

Representative sections submitted in labeled cassettes A1 through A31 per standard Whipple specimen protocol.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

Immunohistochemistry (IHC) — performed on the primary tumor block:
  - CK7: Positive (diffuse, strong) — tumor glands
  - CK19: Positive (diffuse, strong) — tumor glands
  - MUC1: Positive (luminal and cytoplasmic) — tumor glands
  - MUC5AC: Focally positive — subset of tumor glands
  - CA 19-9 (IHC): Positive — tumor glands
  - CK20: Negative — tumor glands
  - CDX2: Negative — tumor glands
  - Synaptophysin: Negative (excludes neuroendocrine component)
  - Chromogranin A: Negative (excludes neuroendocrine component)
  - p53: Aberrant expression — strong diffuse overexpression in tumor nuclei (consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation; correlates with known TP53 p.R175H mutation on NGS)
  - SMAD4 (DPC4): LOSS OF EXPRESSION — complete loss of nuclear SMAD4 staining in tumor cells (internal stromal and endothelial controls retained) consistent with SMAD4 inactivation
  - Ki-67: Approximately 20-25% proliferation index in viable residual tumor cells (decreased from 35% on pre-treatment biopsy, consistent with treatment effect)

Mismatch Repair (MMR) Protein IHC — PENDING (tissue submitted; results to follow in addendum)
  - MLH1: [Pending]
  - PMS2: [Pending]
  - MSH2: [Pending]
  - MSH6: [Pending]
  [Note: Prior EUS-guided biopsy MMR IHC not performed; MSI status by NGS on this specimen submitted for comprehensive genomic profiling — see separate NGS report.]

Molecular/NGS: Tissue from this resection specimen has been submitted for comprehensive genomic profiling (648-gene next-generation sequencing panel). Results reported separately.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Sections of the pancreatic head tumor show a moderately differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma with extensive treatment effect. The tumor is characterized by infiltrative glands and tubular structures, often with angulated, irregular contours set within an extensively desmoplastic and hyalinized stroma. The cancer cells display moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and a moderate amount of pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are infrequent in the post-treatment specimen (1-2 per 10 high-power fields). Large areas of the original tumor bed show acellular hyalinized fibrosis, dystrophic calcification, and small foci of foamy macrophage aggregates, consistent with necrosis and treatment-induced fibrosis. Acellular mucin pools surrounded by fibrotic stroma are present without viable mucin-producing tumor cells; these are scored as necrotic/treatment effect per CAP regression grading criteria.

Residual viable tumor nests are present, predominantly at the periphery of the fibrotic tumor bed and within perineural spaces. The viable tumor component represents less than 50% of the original tumor bed area but is more than isolated single cells or rare small clusters, consistent with CAP Tumor Regression Grade 2 (moderate response).

Perineural invasion is identified in multiple sections, involving large-caliber autonomic nerve bundles within the peripancreatic soft tissue of the uncinate process. This is a recognized adverse pathologic feature in PDAC and is noted for prognostic purposes.

Lymphovascular invasion is not identified on routine H&E staining after examination of multiple levels; IHC for D2-40 (podoplanin) and CD31 was not performed as lymphovascular invasion was not suspected on H&E review.

The one positive lymph node (1/22) contains a 0.4 cm focus of metastatic moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with a central area of fibrosis, also suggesting treatment effect within the nodal deposit. Extranodal extension is not identified.

All other surgical margins are free of invasive carcinoma as described above.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY / DIAGNOSTIC LINE:

Pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy with en bloc SMV resection:
MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, 2.1 cm, WITH EXTENSIVE TREATMENT EFFECT (CAP TUMOR REGRESSION GRADE 2), PANCREATIC HEAD AND UNCINATE PROCESS.
Perineural invasion present. Lymphovascular invasion absent.
Margins: ALL NEGATIVE (R0). Closest margin: SMA/retroperitoneal margin, 1.2 mm.
Lymph nodes: 1/22 positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma.
Pathologic stage: ypT3 ypN1 M0 — AJCC 8th Edition Stage IIB (post-neoadjuvant therapy).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED BY: [Attending Pathologist, Gastrointestinal/Hepatopancreatobiliary Pathology]
SIGNED OUT: [Date]
CASE NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]

ADDENDUM: MMR IHC results pending; addendum to follow upon completion.
